bug_report.summary: Owners dialog header label does not show file name + total count as specified (shows only “Owners List”)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a project in the editor.
Create a new script file (e.g., res://orphan_script.gd) and do not attach it to any scene/node so it has no owners.
In the Script Editor (or FileSystem), right-click the script and select “View Owners”.
expected_behaviors: Dialog window title should not include the owner count (OK).
Inside the dialog, a label should display the file name and total count (e.g., “orphan_script.gd (Total: 0)” or equivalent), even when there are no owners.
When there are no owners, an explicit message should indicate that no owners were found (OK).
actual_behaviors: The dialog’s internal header label displays only “Owners List” and does not include the file name or total count.
The dialog shows “No owners found for: orphan_script.gd”, but the specified file-name + total-count label inside the dialog is missing/incomplete.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Owners dialog footer layout: OK button not properly centered/aligned after resizing narrower (empty placeholder space shown)
steps_to_reproduce: Open any script in FileSystem dock and choose “View Owners”.
Resize the Owners dialog narrower using the right edge resize handle.
expected_behaviors: Dialog buttons (e.g., OK) should remain properly aligned (typically centered or consistently aligned) without leaving an empty placeholder region.
No extra blank button slot/space should appear in the footer when resizing.
actual_behaviors: After resizing narrower, the OK button shifts position and a large empty/unused rectangular area appears next to it (looks like a missing/blank button slot), making the footer layout look broken.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
